description:
Week-1 of the HCL Java CDC program covers Frontend Development.

Duration: ~30 Hours total

Sections:
1. JavaScript (JS) — 12 Hours (Mentor-led)
2. React JS (Frontend Framework) — 18 Hours (Mentor-led)
3. Mock Assessment

Goal: Build a working frontend with ReactJS, consume REST APIs, handle routing and state management.

Mode: ILT with Hands-on
Ownership: College
Faculty: Dr NIVETHITHA V — nivethitha.v@vit.ac.in

Mentor-led session — Duration: 12 Hours

JavaScript is the language of the web. Every browser understands JS natively.

Why it matters for HCL:
• Spring Boot backend serves REST APIs — JS frontend consumes them
• ReactJS is built on JS — you must know JS before React
• Modern JS (ES6+) is used in all production codebases

Topics covered:
1. Basics — Syntax, Variables, Operators
2. DOM Manipulation and Events
3. ES6+ Features (Arrow Functions, Promises, Modules)
4. Fetch API and Asynchronous Programming
5. Introduction to JavaScript Frameworks and Libraries

Status: Week-1 content

SYNTAX & VARIABLES:

var vs let vs const:
• var — function-scoped, hoisted, avoid in modern code
• let — block-scoped, reassignable
• const — block-scoped, cannot be reassigned (but object properties can change)

Data types:
• Primitive: string, number, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, BigInt
• Reference: object, array, function

Type coercion:
• == does type coercion: '5' == 5 → true
• === strict equality: '5' === 5 → false (always use ===)

OPERATORS:
• Arithmetic: + - * / % **
• Comparison: === !== < > <= >=
• Logical: && || ! (short-circuit evaluation)
• Nullish coalescing: ?? (returns right side only if left is null/undefined)
• Optional chaining: obj?.property (safe property access)

KEY CONCEPTS:
• Hoisting: var declarations moved to top of scope at compile time
• Temporal Dead Zone: let/const cannot be accessed before declaration line
• Truthy/Falsy: 0, '', null, undefined, NaN, false are falsy; everything else is truthy

EXAMPLES:
  const name = 'HCL';
  let count = 0;
  count++;
  const result = count > 0 ? 'positive' : 'zero';
  const val = null ?? 'default';  // 'default'
  const len = obj?.name?.length;  // safe access

text: Introduction to JavaScript Frameworks and Libraries
description:
WHY FRAMEWORKS?
Pure JS DOM manipulation becomes unmaintainable at scale. Frameworks provide:
• Component-based UI architecture
• Reactive state management
• Routing, build tooling, testing

MAJOR JS FRAMEWORKS:

• React (Meta) — Component library, virtual DOM, most popular
  Used by: Facebook, Airbnb, Netflix, Uber
  Learn: react.dev

• Angular (Google) — Full MVC framework, TypeScript-first
  Used by: Google, Microsoft, Deutsche Bank
  Learn: angular.dev

• Vue.js (Community) — Progressive framework, gentle learning curve
  Used by: Alibaba, Xiaomi, GitLab
  Learn: vuejs.org

• Svelte — Compiler-based, no virtual DOM, tiny bundle size

KEY CONCEPTS SHARED BY ALL:
• Component: reusable, self-contained UI piece (header, button, form)
• State: data that when changed, re-renders the UI
• Props: data passed from parent to child component
• Lifecycle: component mount → update → unmount
• Routing: client-side URL-to-component mapping

WHY REACT FOR HCL?
• Most popular (65% of JS developer usage)
• Huge ecosystem (Next.js, Redux, React Query)
• Pairs naturally with Spring Boot REST APIs
• Industry standard in enterprise Java shops

BUILD TOOLS:
• Vite — fast dev server and bundler (used in this project)
• Webpack — older, highly configurable bundler
• npm / Yarn — package managers

Introduction to ReactJS and JSX — Mentor-led session, Duration: 18 Hours React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces using components. Created by Facebook/Meta (2013). Powers Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp Web. CORE IDEA: UI = f(state) — given the same state, the UI is always the same VIRTUAL DOM: React keeps a lightweight copy of the DOM in memory. On state change → diff old vs new virtual DOM → update only changed real DOM nodes. This is called reconciliation. JSX: Syntactic sugar for React.createElement(). Babel compiles it. const el =

Hello {name}

; // Compiles to: const el = React.createElement('h1', {className:'title'}, 'Hello ' + name); RULES OF JSX: • className instead of class • htmlFor instead of for • All tags must be closed:
• Return single root element (or Fragment: <>...) • JS expressions in {curly braces}: {2 + 2}, {name}, {condition ? 'a' : 'b'} Topics in this section: 1. Components, Props, and State 2. React Lifecycle Methods and Hooks 3. State Management (Context API, Redux) 4. Routing with React Router 5. Integrating APIs with Axios or Fetch 6. Advanced Topics (Performance Optimization, Error Boundaries) 7. Mini Project Development

COMPONENTS: Function components are the modern standard (class components are legacy). // Function component function Greeting({ name, age }) { return

Hello {name}, age {age}

; } // Usage PROPS: • Props = properties passed from parent to child (read-only in child) • Can be any type: string, number, boolean, object, array, function • Destructure in function signature: function Card({ title, onClick }) • children prop: whatever is between opening and closing tags

Content

 → props.children =

Content

STATE (useState): import { useState } from 'react'; function Counter() { const [count, setCount] = useState(0); // [value, setter] return ( <>

{count}

); } KEY RULES: • Never mutate state directly: setItems([...items, newItem]) not items.push() • State updates are async — don't read state immediately after set • For object state: spread the old state: setUser({...user, name: 'Bob'}) COMPONENT TREE: App ├── Header ├── Main │ ├── Sidebar │ └── Content │ ├── Card │ └── Card └── Footer Data flows DOWN (props). Events flow UP (callback functions passed as props).

text: Integrating APIs with Axios or Fetch

description:
Build a complete ReactJS frontend as a mini project.

SUGGESTED PROJECT IDEAS (from HCL CDC):

1. MealDB App
   • Fetch meals from https://www.themealdb.com/api/json/v1/1/search.php?s=
   • Display meals, search by name, show meal details
   • Find meal with fewest ingredients

2. Rest Countries App
   • Use REST Countries API
   • List all countries, search by name/code/continent/capital
   • Show country details (flag, population, capital)

3. Spotify Clone (simplified)
   • Song search, playlist management
   • Requires Spotify Developer API key

4. Freshdesk Clone
   • Ticket CRUD operations
   • Contact management
   • Use Freshdesk sandbox API

PROJECT CHECKLIST:
□ Create React app with Vite
□ Set up React Router for multiple pages
□ Create reusable components (Card, Button, Modal, Loader)
□ Implement API calls with Axios
□ Handle loading, error, and empty states
□ Add basic form with validation
□ Use useState and useEffect correctly
□ Style with CSS modules or Tailwind
□ Deploy to Netlify or Vercel

FOLDER STRUCTURE:
  src/
  ├── components/   (reusable UI)
  ├── pages/        (route-level components)
  ├── services/     (API calls)
  ├── hooks/        (custom hooks)
  └── utils/        (helper functions)

Frontend Mock Assessment after Week-1.

Expected coverage:
• JavaScript fundamentals (variables, closures, this, event loop)
• DOM manipulation and event handling
• ES6+ syntax (arrow functions, destructuring, spread, async/await)
• Fetch API usage
• React components, props, state
• useEffect, useState hooks
• Simple routing with React Router

FORMAT (typical):
• MCQ on JS/React concepts
• Code snippet analysis (what is the output?)
• Write a React component from requirements
• Debug a given React component

PREP TIPS:
• Practice writing components from scratch without IDE help
• Understand the Event Loop — common MCQ topic
• Know the difference between == and ===, var/let/const
• Understand React re-render triggers
• Be able to implement a basic CRUD UI with React
